stem: Which suture material is most commonly used for bowel anastomosis?
rationale: Absorbable synthetic sutures like Vicryl (braided) or PDS (monofilament) are preferred for bowel anastomosis because they maintain tensile strength during the critical healing period and then absorb, reducing long-term foreign body reaction.

stem: The Allen test is performed before radial artery cannulation to assess:
options: Adequate collateral blood flow through the ulnar artery to the hand
Range of motion of the wrist
rationale: The Allen test verifies adequate collateral circulation via the ulnar artery before radial artery cannulation or harvesting. Both arteries are occluded, then the ulnar is released to see if color returns to the hand.

stem: The diaphragm separates the thoracic and abdominal cavities. Which structures pass through the diaphragm?
options: The esophagus (T10), aorta (T12), and inferior vena cava (T8)
rationale: Three major structures pass through the diaphragm: the IVC through the caval hiatus at T8, the esophagus through the esophageal hiatus at T10, and the aorta through the aortic hiatus at T12.

stem: Vancomycin-resistant Enterococcus (VRE) requires which type of isolation precautions?
options: Contact precautions with gown and gloves
rationale: VRE is transmitted through direct contact with infected patients or contaminated surfaces. Contact precautions require gown and gloves for all patient contact, dedicated equipment, and enhanced environmental cleaning.

stem: During a splenectomy, the surgical technologist should have which specific supply available?
options: Large hemostatic clips and a specimen retrieval bag for the spleen
rationale: The spleen is highly vascular, requiring large clips for the splenic hilum vessels. A specimen bag allows safe retrieval of the spleen, especially in laparoscopic procedures, preventing spillage of splenic tissue.

stem: The minimum time required for high-level disinfection with 2% glutaraldehyde is:
rationale: High-level disinfection with 2% glutaraldehyde requires a minimum of 20 minutes of contact time at room temperature. This kills all vegetative bacteria, mycobacteria, viruses, and fungi but may not eliminate all spores.

stem: The surgical technologist notices the surgeon break sterile technique by touching a non-sterile surface. The correct action is to:
options: Wait until after the procedure to report it
Immediately and respectfully inform the surgeon of the contamination
Ignore it if the contamination seems minor
Document it but take no immediate action
rationale: Surgical conscience requires immediate reporting of any sterile technique break, regardless of who commits it. The contaminated item or area must be managed according to protocol to prevent patient infection.

stem: The quadriceps femoris muscle group includes all EXCEPT:
rationale: The quadriceps has four muscles: rectus femoris, vastus lateralis, vastus medialis, and vastus intermedius. The biceps femoris is part of the hamstring group on the posterior thigh.

stem: The purpose of a chest tube (thoracostomy tube) placed after thoracic surgery is to:
options: Remove air and fluid from the pleural space and re-establish negative pressure
rationale: Chest tubes drain air (pneumothorax) and fluid (hemothorax, pleural effusion) from the pleural space, allowing the lung to re-expand. They connect to a water seal drainage system that maintains negative pleural pressure.

stem: Instrument sets wrapped in woven textile (muslin) wrappers should be wrapped with:
options: Two layers (sequential double wrapping) to create an effective bacterial barrier
No wrapping is needed for textile
rationale: AAMI standards recommend sequential double wrapping with woven textile wrappers to create an adequate microbial barrier. Each wrapper is applied separately to create two independent barrier layers.

stem: The Kocher clamp differs from the Kelly clamp because it has:
options: Teeth at the tip of the jaws for a more secure grip on tough tissue
rationale: The Kocher (Ochsner) clamp has interlocking teeth at the tip that provide a stronger, more secure grip on tough tissue like fascia. Kelly clamps have smooth jaws and are used on vessels and softer tissue.

stem: Capnography during anesthesia monitors:
options: The concentration of carbon dioxide in exhaled gases (end-tidal CO2)
rationale: Capnography provides continuous measurement of exhaled CO2 (ETCO2), which reflects ventilation status and pulmonary blood flow. Normal ETCO2 is 35-45 mmHg. It is essential for confirming endotracheal tube placement.

stem: The internal mammary artery is commonly harvested during coronary artery bypass grafting because:
options: It is longer than other available grafts
It has superior long-term patency rates compared to saphenous vein grafts
rationale: The left internal mammary artery (LIMA) has over 90% patency at 10 years when grafted to the LAD artery, far exceeding saphenous vein grafts (50-60% at 10 years). This makes it the conduit of choice for CABG.

stem: The most effective method of hand hygiene in the OR for routine patient contact is:
options: Wiping hands on a clean towel
Alcohol-based hand rub applied for at least 15 seconds
rationale: Alcohol-based hand rubs (60-95% alcohol) are the preferred method for routine hand hygiene in healthcare. They are more effective, faster, and less irritating than soap and water for hands without visible soil.

stem: The tibial plateau is an important anatomical landmark during:
options: Total knee arthroplasty for positioning the tibial component
rationale: During total knee arthroplasty, the tibial plateau is resected and prepared to receive the tibial tray component. Proper alignment and sizing of the tibial cut are critical for prosthesis function and longevity.

stem: The purpose of a Bier block (IV regional anesthesia) is to:
options: Provide anesthesia to an extremity by injecting local anesthetic into the venous system after tourniquet application
rationale: A Bier block involves exsanguinating the extremity, inflating a tourniquet, and injecting local anesthetic into a distal IV. The tourniquet prevents systemic absorption, providing regional anesthesia for short procedures.

stem: Mesh repair of inguinal hernias provides what advantage over primary tissue repair?
options: Lower recurrence rate by reinforcing the weakened tissue with a prosthetic barrier
rationale: Tension-free mesh repair (Lichtenstein technique) has recurrence rates below 1-2%, compared to 5-15% for primary tissue repairs. The mesh reinforces the inguinal floor without creating tension on the tissue.

stem: The surgical technologist should change the blade on a scalpel handle by:
options: Using a needle holder to grasp the blade and removing or placing it, never using bare fingers
Bending the blade off with hemostats
rationale: Scalpel blades must be loaded and removed using a needle holder or blade remover to prevent sharps injuries. The blade is slid onto or off the handle using the instrument, never with bare fingers.

stem: The thyroid cartilage is commonly known as the:
options: Adams apple, the largest cartilage of the larynx
rationale: The thyroid cartilage is the largest laryngeal cartilage, forming the anterior prominence of the larynx. It serves as a landmark for emergency cricothyrotomy (performed through the cricothyroid membrane below it).

stem: Peracetic acid as a sterilant has the advantage of:
options: Producing non-toxic byproducts (water, oxygen, and acetic acid) and short cycle times
rationale: Peracetic acid is an effective sterilant that produces only water, oxygen, and acetic acid as byproducts. It works at low temperatures with short cycle times, making it suitable for heat-sensitive immersible instruments.

stem: The DeBakey forceps are characterized by:
options: Fine atraumatic tips with closely spaced teeth for handling delicate vascular tissue
rationale: DeBakey forceps have distinctive closely-set rows of fine teeth on the tips that grip tissue firmly without crushing. They are the preferred forceps for handling blood vessels and other delicate tissues.

stem: The WHO Surgical Safety Checklist includes three phases:
options: Sign In (before anesthesia), Time Out (before incision), and Sign Out (before leaving the OR)
rationale: The WHO Surgical Safety Checklist has three critical phases: Sign In (before anesthesia induction), Time Out (before skin incision), and Sign Out (before the patient leaves the OR). Each phase has specific required verification items.

stem: The common bile duct is formed by the junction of the:
options: Right and left hepatic ducts only
Common hepatic duct and the cystic duct
Pancreatic duct and the hepatic duct
rationale: The common bile duct is formed by the union of the common hepatic duct (from the liver) and the cystic duct (from the gallbladder). It carries bile to the duodenum through the ampulla of Vater.

stem: Surgical smoke (plume) generated by electrosurgery and lasers requires:
options: Evacuation using a smoke evacuator to protect OR personnel from harmful particulates and chemicals
rationale: Surgical smoke contains toxic chemicals, dead and live cellular material (including viral DNA), and fine particulates. NIOSH and OSHA recommend smoke evacuation devices within 2 inches of the surgical site.

stem: During an anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF), the surgical approach is through the:
options: Anterior neck along the medial border of the sternocleidomastoid muscle
rationale: The anterior approach to the cervical spine uses an incision along the medial border of the SCM muscle. The carotid sheath is retracted laterally and the trachea/esophagus medially to expose the anterior vertebral bodies.

stem: Ultrasonic cleaners remove soil from surgical instruments by:
options: Creating microscopic cavitation bubbles that implode and dislodge soil from instrument surfaces
rationale: Ultrasonic cleaners generate high-frequency sound waves (20-40 kHz) in a detergent solution, creating cavitation bubbles. When these bubbles implode near surfaces, they generate energy that dislodges soil from crevices and joints.

stem: The Hohmann retractor is used in orthopedic surgery to:
options: Retract soft tissue around bone by hooking around the bone edge
rationale: The Hohmann retractor has a pointed tip that hooks around the edge of bone, with the flat blade retracting surrounding soft tissue. It provides excellent exposure during orthopedic procedures like joint replacements.

stem: ASA physical status classification I indicates:
options: A normal healthy patient with no systemic disease
A patient with severe systemic disease
A patient with mild systemic disease
rationale: ASA I describes a normal, healthy patient with no organic, physiologic, or psychiatric disturbance. The ASA classification helps predict perioperative risk: ASA I has the lowest anesthetic risk.

stem: The vertebral artery enters the skull through the:
rationale: The vertebral arteries ascend through the transverse foramina of the cervical vertebrae and enter the skull through the foramen magnum. They join to form the basilar artery, supplying the posterior brain.

stem: Transmission-based precautions are used in addition to standard precautions when:
options: The pathogen can spread by airborne, droplet, or contact routes beyond what standard precautions can prevent
Standard precautions are sufficient for all situations
rationale: Transmission-based precautions are additional measures for pathogens that require more than standard precautions: airborne (TB, measles), droplet (influenza, pertussis), or contact (MRSA, VRE, C. difficile).

stem: A fasciotomy is performed to:
options: Release compartment pressure in an extremity to prevent tissue necrosis from compartment syndrome
rationale: Fasciotomy involves surgically incising the fascia to relieve elevated compartment pressure. Compartment syndrome occurs when swelling within a rigid fascial compartment compromises blood flow to muscles and nerves.

stem: Washer-decontaminator machines clean instruments by:
options: Using a combination of water jets, detergent, thermal disinfection, and drying cycles
rationale: Automated washer-decontaminators provide standardized cleaning through pre-rinse, enzymatic wash, rinse, thermal disinfection (90-95 degrees C), and drying cycles. They provide consistent results and reduce personnel exposure to contaminated instruments.

stem: The Russian tissue forceps have:
options: A locking mechanism like a hemostat
Broad, round, cupped tips that interlock for grasping heavy tissue without cutting through it
rationale: Russian forceps have distinctive round, cupped, interlocking tips that provide excellent grip on heavy tissue like fascia and muscle without cutting through it. They are commonly used in abdominal and thoracic surgery.

stem: The recovery position (lateral recumbent) is used postoperatively to:
options: Maintain airway patency and prevent aspiration of vomitus
rationale: The recovery (lateral recumbent) position keeps the airway clear by allowing secretions and vomitus to drain from the mouth rather than pooling in the airway. The tongue also falls forward, preventing airway obstruction.

stem: The common peroneal nerve is most vulnerable to injury at the:
options: Neck of the fibula where it wraps around the bone superficially
rationale: The common peroneal nerve wraps around the neck of the fibula just below the skin surface, making it vulnerable to compression from improper positioning, stirrups, or tight bandages. Injury causes foot drop.

stem: Single-use disposable instruments are designed to:
options: Be reprocessed and reused multiple times
Be used once and then discarded, eliminating the risk of cross-contamination from reprocessing failures
rationale: Single-use instruments eliminate cross-contamination risks associated with inadequate reprocessing. They are factory-sterilized and disposed of after one use in sharps or biohazard containers.

stem: During a below-knee amputation, the bone is typically divided at the:
options: Junction of the middle and distal third of the tibia and fibula
rationale: A below-knee amputation (BKA) divides the tibia and fibula at the junction of the middle and distal thirds, allowing sufficient tissue for a well-padded residual limb suitable for prosthetic fitting.

stem: Immediate-use steam sterilization (IUSS) cycles at 270 degrees F gravity displacement for unwrapped items require:
rationale: IUSS gravity displacement cycles at 270 degrees F require 3 minutes for non-porous, non-lumened metal instruments. Porous items require 10 minutes. These times apply to unwrapped items only.

stem: Which hemostatic agent is derived from oxidized regenerated cellulose?
rationale: Surgicel is an absorbable hemostatic agent made from oxidized regenerated cellulose. It is applied to bleeding surfaces where it swells and forms a gel that aids clot formation. It absorbs within 7-14 days.

stem: The surgical technologist should verify the functionality of the electrosurgical unit before each case by:
options: Plugging it in and assuming it works
Having the surgeon test it during the procedure
Testing the settings, ensuring proper pad placement, and checking all connections and alarms
rationale: Pre-case ESU verification includes testing cut and coag modes, confirming alarm function, verifying proper grounding pad placement on the patient, and checking all cable connections for integrity.

stem: The renal hilum contains which structures?
options: Renal artery, renal vein, ureter, lymphatics, and nerves
rationale: The renal hilum is the concave medial border of the kidney where the renal artery enters, renal vein exits, and the ureter and lymphatics emerge. Knowledge of hilar anatomy is critical during nephrectomy.

stem: Enzymatic detergents used for instrument cleaning work by:
options: Breaking down organic soil (blood, tissue, bone) through enzymatic action specific to proteins, fats, and carbohydrates
rationale: Enzymatic cleaners contain protease, lipase, and amylase enzymes that specifically break down proteins, fats, and carbohydrates in organic soil. They must be used at the correct dilution and temperature per manufacturer IFU.

stem: During a thyroidectomy, the parathyroid glands must be preserved because they:
options: Regulate calcium and phosphorus metabolism through parathyroid hormone secretion
rationale: The four parathyroid glands secrete PTH, which regulates serum calcium levels. Inadvertent removal causes hypoparathyroidism with severe hypocalcemia, leading to tetany, seizures, and cardiac arrhythmias.

stem: The proper storage of sterile supplies requires:
options: Clean, dry, temperature-controlled environment with items stored 8-10 inches from the floor and 18 inches from ceiling sprinklers
rationale: Sterile storage areas must be clean, dry, well-ventilated, and temperature-controlled. Items are stored 8-10 inches from the floor, 18 inches from ceiling sprinklers, and 2 inches from walls to prevent contamination.

stem: The Adson tissue forceps differ from the Brown-Adson forceps in that:
options: Adson forceps have one row of teeth (1x2 or 2x3) while Brown-Adson have multiple rows of fine teeth
rationale: Adson forceps have standard teeth (1x2) at the tips for grasping skin during closure. Brown-Adson forceps have multiple rows of fine teeth that provide a wider gripping surface with less tissue trauma.

stem: General anesthesia involves which combination of effects?
options: Unconsciousness, analgesia, amnesia, and muscle relaxation
rationale: General anesthesia provides four effects: unconsciousness (hypnosis), analgesia (pain relief), amnesia (memory loss), and muscle relaxation. Different medications target each component of the anesthetic state.

stem: The piriformis muscle is an important landmark during posterior hip surgery because:
options: It is the only muscle in the hip
It attaches to the femoral head
It is always removed during hip replacement
The sciatic nerve passes inferior to it (or through it in some patients)
rationale: The sciatic nerve typically passes inferior to the piriformis muscle as it exits the pelvis through the greater sciatic notch. The piriformis is a key landmark for identifying and protecting the sciatic nerve during posterior hip approaches.

stem: Sterile saline used for irrigation during surgery must be:
options: Warmed to body temperature (98.6 degrees F/37 degrees C) and verified for correct solution and expiration date
rationale: Irrigation solution should be warmed to body temperature to prevent hypothermia. The technologist verifies the solution type, concentration, and expiration date before dispensing it onto the sterile field.

stem: The Bovie scratch pad (holster) is used to:
options: Clean the electrosurgical tip of carbonized tissue between uses
rationale: The scratch pad (disposable tip cleaner) removes charred tissue buildup from the ESU tip. A clean tip ensures efficient current delivery and reduces the risk of tissue sticking and smoke production.

stem: Traction applied during orthopedic surgery serves to:
options: Maintain bone alignment, reduce fractures, and provide counter-force during reduction and fixation
Increase blood flow to the extremity
rationale: Surgical traction maintains bone alignment and length, assists in fracture reduction, and provides counter-force during hardware placement. It can be applied through skin (Bucks) or bone (skeletal traction with pins).

stem: The surgical technologist should handle tissue specimens:
options: By placing it in formalin before the surgeon examines it
By squeezing it to express fluid
Without labeling until after the case
With care to preserve the tissue in its natural state without crushing or contaminating it
rationale: Specimens must be handled carefully to preserve histological architecture. They should not be squeezed, crushed, or dried out. The surgeon may need to examine the specimen for margins before it is placed in fixative.

stem: The sympathetic nervous system response (fight or flight) causes:
options: Decreased heart rate and constricted pupils
Increased heart rate, dilated pupils, bronchodilation, and elevated blood pressure
rationale: The sympathetic nervous system prepares the body for stress: tachycardia, hypertension, bronchodilation, pupil dilation, decreased GI motility, and increased glucose release. Understanding this helps in recognizing surgical stress responses.

stem: Surgical wound dehiscence refers to:
options: Separation of wound edges after surgical closure
rationale: Wound dehiscence is the partial or complete separation of wound layers after closure. Risk factors include obesity, malnutrition, infection, excessive coughing, and poor surgical technique. Evisceration may occur in abdominal wounds.

stem: During a total shoulder arthroplasty, the humeral head is replaced with:
options: A metal prosthetic humeral head and stem
rationale: In total shoulder arthroplasty, the humeral head is replaced with a metal (cobalt-chrome or titanium) prosthetic head on a stem inserted into the humeral canal. A polyethylene glenoid component replaces the glenoid surface.

stem: The proper technique for removing a soiled gown and gloves after a procedure is:
options: Pull the gown off over the head
Remove gloves first (or gown and gloves together), then remove the gown by turning it inside out, touching only the inside surface
rationale: Gloves are removed first (or simultaneously with the gown) to prevent contaminating clean hands. The gown is removed by turning it inside out, containing the contaminated outer surface within the gown roll.

stem: The Alexander periosteal elevator is specifically designed for:
options: Elevating periosteum from rib surfaces during thoracic procedures
rationale: The Alexander periosteal elevator has a curved blade designed to match the contour of ribs, allowing efficient separation of periosteum during rib resection or thoracotomy procedures.

stem: Dantrolene sodium is the specific treatment for:
options: Malignant hyperthermia - it directly inhibits calcium release from the sarcoplasmic reticulum
rationale: Dantrolene sodium is a skeletal muscle relaxant that directly inhibits calcium release from the sarcoplasmic reticulum, stopping the hypermetabolic cascade of malignant hyperthermia. It must be available wherever general anesthesia is administered.

stem: The portal triad (hepatoduodenal ligament) contains:
options: Common bile duct, hepatic artery proper, and portal vein
rationale: The portal triad within the hepatoduodenal ligament contains the common bile duct (anterolateral), hepatic artery proper (anteromedial), and portal vein (posterior). The Pringle maneuver compresses this triad to control hepatic bleeding.

stem: The incubation period of a biological indicator should not exceed:
options: 24 hours for rapid-read indicators or 24-48 hours for conventional spore tests
rationale: Conventional biological indicators require 24-48 hours of incubation to detect spore growth. Rapid-read indicators use enzyme-based technology to provide results in 1-3 hours, allowing faster release of sterilized loads.

stem: During a laparoscopic Nissen fundoplication, the first step after port placement is:
options: Dividing the short gastric vessels first
Identification and retraction of the left lobe of the liver to expose the gastroesophageal junction
rationale: After establishing pneumoperitoneum and port placement, the left hepatic lobe is retracted using a liver retractor to expose the esophageal hiatus and gastroesophageal junction for the fundoplication.

stem: Rigid sterilization containers must be inspected before each use for:
options: Intact gaskets, functional valve filters, latch integrity, and absence of dents or cracks
rationale: Container integrity depends on functional gaskets (seals), valve filters (allow steam penetration while maintaining sterility), working latches, and undamaged walls. Any compromised component can lead to sterilization failure.

stem: The Debakey-type aortic clamps are designed with:
options: Long jaws with multiple rows of fine teeth for clamping the aorta without damaging the vessel wall
rationale: DeBakey aortic clamps have long, angled jaws with multiple rows of fine atraumatic teeth that distribute clamping pressure evenly across the vessel wall, preventing intimal damage to the aorta.

stem: Preoperative fasting (NPO) guidelines for adults typically require nothing to eat for:
options: At least 6-8 hours before general anesthesia to reduce aspiration risk
rationale: ASA fasting guidelines recommend at least 6-8 hours of NPO for solid food and 2 hours for clear liquids before general anesthesia. This reduces gastric volume and the risk of pulmonary aspiration.

stem: The cauda equina is located in the:
options: Lumbar spinal canal below L1-L2 where the spinal cord ends
rationale: The spinal cord ends at the conus medullaris (L1-L2 in adults). Below this, the nerve roots continue as the cauda equina (horse tail) within the lumbar cistern. Compression causes cauda equina syndrome, a surgical emergency.

stem: Surgical hand antisepsis using an alcohol-based hand rub requires:
options: Clean, dry hands with short nails, followed by application of the product to hands and forearms for the manufacturer-specified duration
rationale: Alcohol-based surgical hand rubs require prewashing if hands are visibly soiled, clean short nails, and dry hands. The product is applied and rubbed over all surfaces of hands and forearms until dry.

stem: A Penfield dissector is used in neurosurgery to:
options: Gently dissect and separate neural tissue and dura from bone or tumor
rationale: Penfield dissectors are blunt, graduated instruments used for gentle dissection of neural tissue, separation of dura from bone, and manipulation of structures in the confined spaces of the cranium and spine.

stem: Autoclave tape (indicator tape) placed on the outside of a wrapped instrument tray:
options: Confirms that the contents are sterile
Replaces the need for biological indicators
Indicates that the package has been through the sterilization process but does not confirm sterility
rationale: External chemical indicator tape (Class 1) changes color (usually diagonal stripes appear) to show the package was exposed to the sterilization process. It is a process indicator, not a sterility indicator.

stem: The Harrington rod system was originally designed for correction of:
options: Spinal scoliosis by providing distraction and compression forces along the spine
rationale: The Harrington rod system was the first widely used posterior spinal instrumentation for scoliosis correction. It uses distraction rods on the concave side and compression rods on the convex side of the curve.

stem: The reverse Trendelenburg position is used to:
options: Improve venous return from the lower extremities
Facilitate upper abdominal and head/neck procedures by shifting abdominal contents inferiorly
rationale: Reverse Trendelenburg (head up, feet down) allows gravity to move abdominal viscera caudally, improving exposure of upper abdominal and diaphragmatic structures. It is also used for laparoscopic upper GI procedures.

stem: The inguinal ligament extends from the:
options: Greater trochanter to the pubic tubercle
Anterior superior iliac spine (ASIS) to the pubic tubercle
rationale: The inguinal (Poupart) ligament is the inferior rolled edge of the external oblique aponeurosis, extending from the ASIS to the pubic tubercle. It forms the floor of the inguinal canal.

stem: Process challenge devices (PCDs) used in sterilization monitoring are designed to:
options: Simulate the most difficult sterilization challenge to verify that sterilization conditions were met throughout the load
rationale: PCDs contain a biological or chemical indicator within a device that creates a defined resistance to sterilant penetration, representing the most difficult sterilization challenge in the load.

stem: During a hemorrhoidectomy, the patient is typically positioned in:
options: Jackknife (prone Kraske) or lithotomy position for perineal access
rationale: The prone jackknife position provides excellent exposure of the perineal area for hemorrhoidectomy. The buttocks are taped apart, and the table is flexed to elevate the pelvis. Lithotomy is an alternative.

stem: The wicking effect in sterile packaging refers to:
options: Moisture traveling through the wrapping material by capillary action, potentially allowing bacterial contamination
rationale: Wicking occurs when moisture on the outside of a sterile package is drawn through the wrapping material by capillary action, creating a pathway for bacteria to contaminate the sterile contents (strike-through contamination).

stem: The Toomey syringe is used in surgery for:
options: Irrigation and aspiration, particularly during urological and arthroscopic procedures
rationale: The Toomey syringe is a large-bore (60 mL) syringe with a wide tip designed for irrigation and aspiration of fluid and debris during urological procedures (bladder irrigation) and arthroscopic washout.

stem: Proper patient skin preparation includes all EXCEPT:
options: Assessing the skin for rashes or infections
Cleaning the skin with an approved antiseptic agent
Shaving the surgical site with a razor the night before surgery
Removing jewelry from the surgical area
rationale: Shaving with a razor the night before increases SSI risk due to micro-cuts that allow bacterial colonization. If hair removal is needed, clipping immediately before surgery is preferred.

stem: The splenic flexure of the colon is located at the junction of:
options: The transverse colon and descending colon, near the spleen and left kidney
rationale: The splenic (left colic) flexure is the sharp bend between the transverse and descending colon, located near the spleen and left kidney. It is higher than the hepatic flexure and attached to the diaphragm by the phrenocolic ligament.

stem: The purpose of the Cidex OPA test strip is to:
options: Verify the minimum effective concentration of OPA disinfectant solution before use
rationale: OPA test strips verify that the minimum effective concentration (MEC) of ortho-phthalaldehyde solution has not dropped below the level required for high-level disinfection. Testing is required before each reuse cycle.

stem: A Charnley hip prosthesis is an example of:
options: A cemented total hip replacement system with a small femoral head for low friction arthroplasty
rationale: Sir John Charnley developed the low-friction arthroplasty concept using a small (22mm) metallic femoral head articulating with a polyethylene cup, fixed with PMMA cement. This design remains a benchmark in hip replacement.

stem: The sterile field should be set up:
options: As close to the time of use as possible, and never left unattended
Only after the patient is asleep
rationale: The sterile field should be created as close to the time of use as practical to minimize the risk of contamination. Once established, it must be continuously monitored and never left unattended.

stem: The Lane bone-holding forceps are used to:
options: Grasp and hold bone fragments in alignment during fracture reduction and fixation
rationale: Lane bone-holding forceps have sharp-pointed jaws that grip bone fragments firmly, maintaining alignment during fracture reduction while plates and screws are applied. They are essential orthopedic instruments.

stem: The proper disposal of sharps in the operating room requires:
options: Leaving them on the Mayo stand until the end of the case
Immediate disposal in a puncture-resistant sharps container at the point of use
rationale: OSHA requires immediate disposal of sharps in approved puncture-resistant containers at the point of use. Needles should not be bent, broken, or recapped. Containers must be replaced when three-quarters full.

stem: The gluteal region receives its blood supply primarily from the:
options: Superior and inferior gluteal arteries, branches of the internal iliac artery
rationale: The superior and inferior gluteal arteries (branches of the internal iliac artery) supply the gluteal muscles. They exit the pelvis through the greater sciatic foramen, above and below the piriformis muscle.

stem: Water quality for instrument reprocessing should be:
options: Treated, filtered, and meeting AAMI standards to prevent mineral deposits and bacterial contamination
rationale: AAMI standards specify water quality requirements for instrument reprocessing. Treated water (deionized, distilled, or RO) prevents mineral deposits on instruments and bacterial contamination from biofilm in water lines.

stem: A gastrectomy involves removal of:
options: All or part of the stomach
rationale: Gastrectomy is surgical removal of all (total gastrectomy) or part (subtotal/partial gastrectomy) of the stomach. Indications include gastric cancer, severe ulcer disease, and bariatric surgery.

stem: The aeration phase after ethylene oxide sterilization is necessary to:
options: Remove toxic ETO residuals from sterilized items to safe levels before patient contact
rationale: ETO is toxic and mutagenic. Aeration removes residual ETO and its byproducts (ethylene glycol, ethylene chlorohydrin) from sterilized items. Aeration times vary from 8-12 hours at elevated temperatures.

stem: The Lahey clamp (thyroid clamp) is designed for:
options: Clamping blood vessels in the leg
Grasping the thyroid gland tissue with its serrated jaws during thyroidectomy
rationale: The Lahey clamp has serrated jaws specifically designed for grasping thyroid tissue. Its small size and precise grip allow controlled manipulation of the thyroid gland during dissection.

stem: Regional anesthesia includes all EXCEPT:
rationale: Regional anesthesia provides numbness to a specific body region without loss of consciousness. It includes spinal, epidural, and peripheral nerve blocks. General anesthesia renders the patient unconscious.

stem: The iliac crest is the most common site for harvesting:
rationale: The iliac crest provides a large volume of cancellous and cortical bone for autogenous grafting. It is easily accessible and provides high-quality graft material for spinal fusion, fracture repair, and reconstructive procedures.

stem: The minimum exposure time for steam sterilization at 250 degrees F (121 degrees C) in a gravity displacement sterilizer for wrapped items is:
rationale: At 250 degrees F (121 degrees C), gravity displacement sterilization requires 30 minutes for wrapped/packaged items. Higher temperatures (270 degrees F/132 degrees C) reduce the time to 15 minutes for gravity and 4 minutes for prevacuum.

stem: Hand hygiene compliance is the single most important measure to prevent:
rationale: The CDC and WHO identify hand hygiene as the single most important practice for preventing healthcare-associated infections. Compliance with hand hygiene protocols significantly reduces pathogen transmission.

stem: During a Bankart repair of the shoulder, the surgical team addresses:
options: A detachment of the anterior-inferior glenoid labrum from the glenoid rim
rationale: A Bankart lesion is a tear of the anterior-inferior glenoid labrum caused by anterior shoulder dislocation. Bankart repair reattaches the labrum to the glenoid rim using suture anchors to prevent recurrent dislocation.

stem: The purpose of the neutral zone during surgery is to:
options: Provide a designated area for safe transfer of sharp instruments without hand-to-hand passing
rationale: The neutral zone (hands-free zone) is a designated area where sharp instruments are placed for transfer between team members, eliminating direct hand-to-hand passing and reducing the risk of percutaneous injury.

stem: The difference between monofilament and braided suture is:
options: Monofilament cannot be used for internal suturing
Monofilament is a single strand causing less tissue drag; braided is multiple strands with better knot security but higher infection risk
rationale: Monofilament suture (single strand) has less tissue drag, lower infection risk (no interstices for bacteria), but poorer knot security. Braided suture has better handling and knot security but may harbor bacteria in its interstices.

stem: The pleural space normally contains:
options: A small amount of serous fluid (5-15 mL) that lubricates the pleural surfaces during breathing
rationale: The pleural space normally contains 5-15 mL of serous fluid between the visceral and parietal pleura. This fluid allows frictionless lung movement during respiration. Abnormal accumulation causes pleural effusion.

stem: Load configuration in a steam sterilizer should:
options: Stack heavy items on top of lighter ones
Pack items tightly to maximize capacity
Place items directly against chamber walls
Allow free circulation of steam throughout all items, with packages placed on edge and not touching the chamber walls
rationale: Proper load configuration ensures steam contacts all surfaces. Packages are placed on edge, not compressed, with space between items and chamber walls. Overloading prevents steam penetration and sterilization failure.

stem: Contact time is critical for disinfection. Surfaces cleaned with a hospital-grade disinfectant must remain wet for:
options: The manufacturer-specified contact time (typically 1-10 minutes depending on the product)
rationale: Disinfectant effectiveness depends on adequate contact time with the surface. The surface must remain wet for the entire manufacturer-specified kill time. Wiping dry before this time renders the disinfection incomplete.

stem: During a radical prostatectomy, the surgical team must take care to preserve:
options: The neurovascular bundles adjacent to the prostate to maintain erectile function and urinary continence
rationale: The neurovascular bundles run along the posterolateral aspect of the prostate. Nerve-sparing technique preserves these bundles to maintain erectile function and improve urinary continence outcomes.

stem: The purpose of the Bair Hugger warming system is to:
options: Actively warm the patient using forced-air convective warming to prevent perioperative hypothermia
rationale: The Bair Hugger uses a disposable blanket with forced warm air to maintain patient normothermia. Perioperative hypothermia increases infection risk, bleeding, cardiac complications, and prolongs recovery.

stem: The internal oblique muscle of the abdominal wall is the:
options: Middle of the three flat abdominal muscles, lying between the external oblique and transversus abdominis
Only muscle in the posterior abdominal wall
rationale: The internal oblique is the middle layer of the three anterolateral abdominal wall muscles. Its fibers run superiorly and medially (opposite to the external oblique), contributing to the rectus sheath and inguinal canal.

stem: The proper method for transporting sterile instrument trays is:
options: Placing them on the floor of the elevator
On a covered cart with the trays secured and protected from contamination during transport
rationale: Sterile items must be transported in a manner that protects them from contamination. Covered carts prevent exposure to airborne contaminants, moisture, and physical damage during transport from sterile processing to the OR.
